acceptance_criteria: `research:` block schema defined and documented in the profile.yaml docs.
Validator rejects malformed `research:` blocks with actionable error messages.
A storage abstraction exists that can target a sibling research repo on either GitHub or ADO without code changes per repo.
Unit tests cover schema validation and storage-abstraction selection per platform combination.
A fresh repo can opt in by adding only the `research:` block — no code changes required.
description:
Establish the foundation other phases stand on.

**Scope**
- Define and validate a new `research:` block in [.polyphony-config/profile.yaml](.polyphony-config/profile.yaml) declaring the archive repo, branch, and auth.
- Build a cross-platform research-storage abstraction that reuses polyphony's existing platform-router pattern so source-on-ADO ↔ research-on-GitHub (and vice versa) is at least architecturally supported.
- Surface clear schema validation errors when the block is missing/malformed.

**Out of scope**
- Any agent logic (assistant/researcher/archivist) — that is Issues 2–4.
- Cross-platform proof-by-demo (Stretch on the Epic).

**Notes for planner**
- Look at the existing platform-router pattern before designing; do not invent a parallel abstraction.
- Keep the schema minimal but extensible (future fields for archive metadata).
title: Research storage abstraction & `research:` profile block

acceptance_criteria: A research sub-workflow is registered and invocable from a parent architect workflow.
`research-assistant` agent reads the kept-archive and emits a structured findings document with citations (source URL + capture date).
Findings are written to scratch under `research/scratch/<run-id>/` on the feature branch.
Workflow can be exercised end-to-end with a mocked archive in tests.
Citations include source URL and capture date (Minimum bar).
description:
Stand up the research sub-workflow and the lightweight first-pass agent.

**Scope**
- New conductor sub-workflow under [.conductor/registry/workflows/](.conductor/registry/workflows/) that any architect can invoke.
- `research-assistant` agent: lightweight model, archive-only first pass; surfaces relevant articles already in the kept-archive.
- Scratch storage on the feature branch under `research/scratch/<run-id>/` (pruning belongs to architect-integration / close-out, not this Issue).
- Findings document format that downstream architects can consume.

**Out of scope**
- Sufficiency judgement and escalation — Issue 3.
- Curation/keep decisions — Issue 4.
- Architect-side `research_needs` emission and loop-back routing — Issue 5.
title: Research sub-workflow & `research-assistant` agent

acceptance_criteria: `researcher` agent makes an explicit sufficient/escalate decision on each pass with a recorded rationale.
`deep-researcher` agent runs only on escalation and has access to the extended toolset.
Escalation is bounded (no unbounded loop) and the bound is configurable or documented.
Tests cover both the no-escalation and escalation paths.
description:
Layer the cost-tiered escalation on top of the assistant first pass.

**Scope**
- `researcher` agent: judges whether the assistant's findings answer the research_needs; orchestrates escalation when not.
- `deep-researcher` agent: extended tools (web search, GitHub search, MCP-provided integrations); invoked only when the researcher escalates.
- Bounded escalation policy so the pipeline cannot loop indefinitely.

**Out of scope**
- Curation decisions (archivist) — Issue 4.
- Integrating the pipeline into the architect loop — Issue 5.
title: `researcher` sufficiency check & `deep-researcher` escalation

acceptance_criteria: `archivist` runs at the end of each research sub-workflow invocation.
Each scratch article receives a recorded keep / discard / expand decision with rationale.
Kept articles are written into the configured sibling research repo via the storage abstraction from Issue 1.
Tests cover all three decisions and the sibling-repo write path.
description:
Add the curation step that runs at the end of every research pass.

**Scope**
- `archivist` agent: decides keep / discard / expand for each scratch article, judging relevance to domain, codebase, technology stacks, ecosystem, and linkability.
- Sibling-repo writer that graduates kept articles into the configured research repo as Obsidian-friendly markdown.
- Hand-off contract for scratch lifecycle (creation in Issue 2; pruning at apex close in Issue 5 or as a follow-on).

**Out of scope**
- Johnny-Decimal numbering, Obsidian polish, INDEX.md maintenance — Issue 6.
- Architect-side wiring — Issue 5.
title: `archivist` curation pass — keep / discard / expand

acceptance_criteria: Architects can emit a structured `research_needs` block as part of their normal output.
Workflow routing detects `research_needs`, invokes the research sub-workflow, and re-enters the same architect with the findings document available.
Research output bypasses the plan-review gate (verified by workflow tests).
End-to-end demo: an architect declares `research_needs`, receives synthesized findings, and produces a refined plan in its next iteration.
Loop is bounded so a misbehaving architect cannot trigger infinite research cycles.
description:
Wire the subsystem into the existing planning workflows so any architect at any depth can request research and resume.

**Scope**
- Architect prompt / output schema gains a structured `research_needs` field.
- Routing in the relevant workflows under [.conductor/registry/workflows/](.conductor/registry/workflows/) so when an architect emits `research_needs`, the research sub-workflow runs and its output feeds the architect's next iteration **without** going through the plan-review gate.
- Apex close-out wires scratch pruning under `research/scratch/<run-id>/` (Stretch unless trivially tucked in).
- End-to-end demo on a real planning iteration (this Epic's own children may serve, after the subsystem exists).

**Out of scope**
- Wiki polish — Issue 6.
- Cross-platform proof — Stretch.
title: Architect integration: `research_needs` emission & review-bypass loop

acceptance_criteria: New kept articles receive a Johnny-Decimal ID and conform to the documented Obsidian frontmatter / wiki-link conventions.
`INDEX.md` is updated atomically with each keep/expand decision.
Citations include a freshness signal in addition to URL + capture date (Stretch — document if deferred).
A short README in the research repo explains the JD scheme and Obsidian conventions.
description:
Tighten the kept-archive into a discoverable wiki. Most items here are Stretch on the Epic; deliver what fits.

**Scope**
- Johnny-Decimal numbering scheme for kept articles.
- Obsidian compatibility: wiki-links, frontmatter conventions.
- Citation rigor: at minimum URL + capture date (Minimum, also covered in Issue 2); freshness signal added here as Stretch.
- `INDEX.md` maintenance: archivist updates a topical index with JD IDs and brief descriptions on each keep/expand.

**Out of scope**
- Migrating existing in-tree docs into the wiki (out of scope on the Epic).
title: Wiki polish: Johnny-Decimal, Obsidian, citations, `INDEX.md`
